abstract: Abstract There is an increasing body of research on algorithmic management (AM), but the field lacks measurement tools to capture workers' experiences of this phenomenon. Based on existing literature, we developed and validated the algorithmic management questionnaire (AMQ) to measure the perceptions of workers regarding their level of exposure to AM. Across three samples (overall n = 1332 gig workers), we show the content, factorial, discriminant, convergent, and predictive validity of the scale. The final 20‐item scale assesses workers' perceived level of exposure to algorithmic: monitoring, goal setting, scheduling, performance rating, and compensation. These dimensions formed a higher order construct assessing overall exposure to algorithmic management, which was found to be, as expected, negatively related to the work characteristics of job autonomy and job complexity and, indirectly, to work engagement. Supplementary analyses revealed that perceptions of exposure to AM reflect the objective presence of AM dimensions beyond individual variations in exposure. Overall, the results suggest the suitability of the AMQ to assess workers' perceived exposure to algorithmic management, which paves the way for further research on the impacts of these rapidly accelerating systems.

ob.ai_reason: 算法管理问卷开发，AI为核心议题
ob.tldr: 研究通过六阶段量表开发流程，构建了20题的算法管理问卷（AMQ），测量零工工作者对算法监控、目标设定、排班、绩效评定和薪酬五个维度的感知暴露度。跨三样本验证显示量表具良好信效度，暴露度与工作自主性、复杂性负相关，并通过工作投入间接影响员工幸福感。结果表明AMQ可有效捕捉工人对算法管理的客观与主观体验，为后续研究提供可靠工具。

title: Creation of the algorithmic management questionnaire: A six‐phase scale development process
title_zh: 算法管理问卷的创建：六阶段量表开发过程
